title: Revert "logformatter: anchors: link to test summary, not name"
body:
It looks like tell/seek don't work in CI-land: important test log
information is being lost. Revert this commit, maybe some day I'll
come up with a better solution.

This reverts commit 1bff0108f6275cc8290e649833f3a194cd6e9ee1.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
If you want a seek-able file we could switch the CI to do make localintegration > tmpfile and then you can run logformatter on that file afterwards and use seek without issues.

Then, when tests hang and time out, we get zero output.

I'm going to look into @cevich's suggestion re: saving json artifacts. ITM, the effect of reverting this is that clicking a test link becomes slightly less friendlier... but at least it includes the test name. Right now, test names are being silently eaten.

> Then, when tests hang and time out, we get zero output.

Could do a `localintegration | tee tmpfile` as a compromise.

>Could do a localintegration | tee tmpfile as a compromise.

Defeats the purpose of logformatter, because we then lose timing information. (Not that timing information is that great, but it's better than the absolute zero information we get from a flatfile).

I recommend just trusting that this PR is Good Enough, and letting me work on a better solution for later.

Confirmation: here is a [log showing desired results](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6570547547471872/html/int-podman-debian-12-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--podman-run-user-capabilities-test--1) (green test name, then, lower down below, a yellow skipped test). Compare to [broken results](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6620238104494080/html/int-podman-debian-12-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-run-user-capabilities-test--1), where the test names have been gobbled.

I thought I had confirmed that `tell/seek` was working in CI, but obviously missed something.

/lgtm
Lets get this in to fix the broken logs.
